$schemaNote: Informal schema (no external JSON Schema file). Each node: id/name/layer/cluster/responsibility/filesOwned/publicInterfaces/upstreamDependencies/downstreamConsumers/sharedState/criticality/criticalityRationale/requiredRegressionTests. Edges are {id: <node id in this file>} or {external: <name>, note: <why it's not its own node>}. See docs/EXECUTION_GRAPH.md for the prose version, the Mermaid diagram, and parallel-work rules this file doesn't repeat.

generatedFrom.method: filesOwned/publicInterfaces/requiredRegressionTests.coLocatedUnitTests/crossCuttingOrIntegrationTests were extracted programmatically (export-statement regex + test-file glob + import-string grep) against the files listed in docs/EXECUTION_GRAPH.md's Subsystem table. layer/cluster/responsibility/upstream/downstream/sharedState/criticality/ciRegressionGuards were authored from docs/EXECUTION_GRAPH.md, docs/ARCHITECTURAL_DEBT.md, docs/PORTFOLIO_OS_V2_SPEC.md Part 5, and src/architecture/regressionGuards.test.ts.

criticalityLevels.critical: Writes or directly derives primary financial state (shares, cost basis, cash) in the replay/write path. A defect is a silent, compounding money-correctness bug — the class of incident named repeatedly in docs/ROADMAP.md.
criticalityLevels.high: Gates, corrects, or orchestrates what reaches a critical node, or aggregates critical nodes into a load-bearing decision. A defect causes a critical node to receive wrong input or lets a wrong verdict reach the user undetected.
criticalityLevels.medium: Derived, read-only, or observability output. A defect is visible and correctable at read time; it never mutates stored state.
criticalityLevels.low: Isolated leaf ingestion (gated by a critical/high node downstream before it can affect stored state) or purely additive, no-op-by-default instrumentation.

nodes.name: Fact Store writes
nodes.responsibility: Append-only creation of RawTransaction facts from parsed import evidence, with identity dedup against already-live facts.

upstreamDependencies.via: authority rank stamped onto facts at write time

sharedState: rawTransactions (append-only, structurally enforced — RawTransactionRepository exposes no update/delete)
nodes.criticalityRationale: The single entry point for every financial fact in the system; a defect here (e.g. a missed dedup check) is the historical root cause class named repeatedly in ROADMAP.md (duplicate execution facts, ARCC/backfill incident).

ciRegressionGuards: one canonical execution fact per business execution identity — every BuyExecution/SellExecution constructor site is in the known, reviewed writer set

nodes.name: Ledger Engine
nodes.responsibility: Pure replay: RawTransaction[] -> LedgerEvent[] for one (portfolio, ticker).
nodes.criticalityRationale: Canonical replay stage that turns facts into ledger events; every downstream position/cash number is derived from its output.

ciRegressionGuards: generateLedgerEvents stays defined in exactly one file (ledgerEngine.ts)

nodes.name: Allocation Engine
nodes.responsibility: Pure replay: LedgerEvent[] -> Allocation[] (which sell closed which lot).
nodes.criticalityRationale: Implements ADR-002 (explicit lot allocation, never FIFO/avg-cost) — the core product invariant. A bug here misattributes realized P/L per lot.

ciRegressionGuards: generateAllocations stays defined in exactly one file (allocationEngine.ts)

nodes.name: Holdings Engine
nodes.responsibility: Pure replay: Ledger + Allocations -> current per-ticker positions (computeHoldings).
nodes.criticalityRationale: One of the 3 CI-frozen position-computation functions; directly feeds the canonical holdings number shown to users.

ciRegressionGuards: computePositions/computeHoldings/computeCanonicalPositions stay the only three position-computation functions

nodes.name: Commit Engine
nodes.responsibility: Orchestrates verify -> replay -> cache for one (portfolio, ticker); the only writer of CommittedLedgerRepository. Owns the (portfolioId, ticker) serialization lock.

upstreamDependencies.note: shared leaf helper (timesConflict) — not its own node

sharedState: ledgerCache, allocationsCache (full delete-and-replace per key, never patched); serialized per `${portfolioId}|${ticker}` via serialize.ts
nodes.criticalityRationale: The single orchestration point where facts become the cached ledger everything else reads; recursive-lock and partial-commit failure modes are named, historical incident classes (ROADMAP.md).

nodes.name: Verification Engine
nodes.responsibility: Judges each fact Verified/Rejected/Needs Review; computes per-ticker match status (checkTickerMatch).
nodes.criticalityRationale: Gates whether a fact is trusted; wrong verdicts don't corrupt storage directly but drive Commit Engine's replay decisions and every user-facing verification banner.

ciRegressionGuards: every known trust/authority/verification-judgment function stays defined in exactly the one file that owns it (verifyAll/verifyTransaction/verifyAllDetailed/verifyTicker, checkTickerMatch)

nodes.name: Completeness Engine
nodes.responsibility: Builds minimal-document recovery plans for evidence gaps.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Pure, read-only advisory output (a recovery-plan suggestion); wrong output misleads the user's next upload but never mutates stored data.

nodes.name: Evidence Graph
nodes.responsibility: Read-only composed view: transaction/document/ticker-position nodes plus corroborate/contradict/missing edges.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Deliberately unpersisted, rebuilt on every read; a bug is visible in the Diagnostics Center UI and correctable without any stored-state risk.

nodes.name: Reconciliation
nodes.responsibility: Compares ledger position to PositionVerification ground truth; runs the ticker-wide reconciliation sweep re-commit.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Drives the mismatch/shortfall banners users act on and the sweep's re-commit trigger; wrong output can mask a real data gap or falsely flag a clean ledger.

ciRegressionGuards: isTickerFullyOfficialBrokerExcelSourced stays defined in exactly reconciliation.ts

nodes.name: Evidence Authority
nodes.responsibility: Canonical trust/authority ranking (authorityRank, higherAuthority) across document/source types.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Every trust decision in the system reduces to this ranking; a wrong rank silently changes which document wins a conflict everywhere at once (the exact bug class this file was created to close).

ciRegressionGuards: the only file allowed to define an AUTHORITY_RANK/SOURCE_RANK/TRUST_RANK-shaped table

nodes.name: Constraint Validation
nodes.responsibility: Inventory/completeness contradiction checks (buildInventoryFacts, evaluateInventoryConstraint, diagnoseInventoryContradiction).
nodes.criticalityRationale: Feeds Verification Engine's constraint report; a wrong contradiction verdict can hide (or falsely raise) a real inventory gap.

nodes.name: TradeService (legacy projection)
nodes.responsibility: Use-case layer for Buy/Sell/delete/move on Trade/TradeAllocation; computePositions (legacy holdings read).
nodes.criticalityRationale: One of two direct writers of the legacy Trade/TradeAllocation tables that most of the UI still reads through canonicalHoldings' fallback path; also one of the six cash writers.

ciRegressionGuards: one of the known direct writers of Trade/TradeAllocation (frozen allowlist)
one of the 3 frozen position-computation functions (computePositions)

nodes.name: ledgerProjection.ts
nodes.responsibility: Rewrites trades/tradeAllocations rows from replayed facts (the second, disclosed dual-writer).
sharedState: trades, tradeAllocations (same FROZEN dual-writer pair as trade-service)
nodes.criticalityRationale: The replay-side writer of the same frozen dual-writer pair as TradeService; disagreement between the two is the exact class of incident the canonicalHoldings fallback exists to catch.

ciRegressionGuards: one of the known direct writers of Trade/TradeAllocation (frozen allowlist)

nodes.name: canonicalHoldings.ts
nodes.responsibility: Hybrid read: serves canonical (replay-derived) holdings when they agree with legacy TradeService, else falls back per-ticker with a disclosed reason.
nodes.criticalityRationale: The production read cutover's safety net — every Dashboard/PortfolioDetail/Portfolios number goes through this; a bug here surfaces as wrong headline numbers even though nothing it reads is itself wrong.

ciRegressionGuards: one of the 3 frozen position-computation functions (computeCanonicalPositions)

nodes.name: ledgerRebuild.ts
nodes.responsibility: Alternate, Upload-based reconstruction with dry-run/apply split; the one deliberately-kept second replay pipeline.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Auto-applies corrections directly to Trade rows; also the one CI-frozen exception to 'only the Ledger/Allocation Engines replay' — any third replay-shaped pipeline appearing elsewhere is the exact bug class this file's own genesis fixed.

ciRegressionGuards: one of the known direct writers of Trade/TradeAllocation (frozen allowlist)
dryRunLedgerRebuild stays defined in exactly one file

nodes.name: lotManager.ts
nodes.responsibility: Ticker-scoped manual sell/allocation UI backend (Lot Manager); home of the identity-collision cross-sell fix.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Direct manual-allocation entry point outside the import-verification gate (by design); the identity-collision bug it fixed was a real cross-sell corruption incident.

ciRegressionGuards: one of the known direct writers of Trade/TradeAllocation (frozen allowlist)

nodes.name: PortfolioService.ts
nodes.responsibility: Deposits/withdrawals/cash adjustments/dividends/splits/rights-issues/archiving.
nodes.criticalityRationale: One of the six direct writers of Portfolio.cash (ARCHITECTURAL_DEBT.md 1.2, still OPEN, no projection live) — a cash bug here is immediately user-visible as a wrong balance with no reconciliation net under it yet.

nodes.name: pendingExecutions.ts
nodes.responsibility: Partial-fill executions awaiting invoice confirmation.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Gates whether a partial fill becomes a confirmed execution; leaf node with no upstream, but its output feeds directly into Fact Store writes once confirmed.

nodes.name: systemValidation.ts / systemSnapshot.ts
nodes.responsibility: Determinism/observability: cross-checks TradeService vs. Holdings Engine; exports a 7-category state hash.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Read-only itself, but is the only structural proof this codebase has that two independent replay runs stay byte-identical — the golden-reference determinism gate everything else's correctness claim rests on.

nodes.name: Analytics Engine
nodes.responsibility: Pure metrics: win rate, profit factor, exposure, health score, sector allocation, etc.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Zero repository dependency, pure functions over an input struct; a wrong metric is visible on the Analytics page and never mutates stored data.

nodes.name: Backup Service
nodes.responsibility: Full-ledger export/import (versioned JSON snapshot); import is always a full replace, never a merge.
sharedState: portfolios, trades, tradeAllocations, timelineEvents, journalEntries, verifications (full replace on import)
nodes.criticalityRationale: A bulk writer of six core tables with a full-replace semantics — a defect here can silently discard or corrupt an entire ledger in one action, the largest single blast radius of any node.

ciRegressionGuards: one of the known direct writers of Trade/TradeAllocation (disclosed exception — bulk import/export, not a business write path)

nodes.name: OCR/Import Pipeline
nodes.responsibility: File -> ParsedTradeCandidate/ParsedDividendCandidate/ParsedOrderEvidence, entirely client-side.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Every candidate it emits passes through Fact Store writes' identity dedup and Verification Engine's judgment before affecting stored state — a parse defect here surfaces as a bad candidate, not silent data corruption.

nodes.name: STES Workbook Import
nodes.responsibility: Frozen AI-exchange .xlsx contract (STES v1.1) -> same candidate shapes as OCR.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Same reasoning as OCR/Import Pipeline — gated by Fact Store writes/Verification Engine downstream.

nodes.name: Thndr Orders Workbook
nodes.responsibility: Account-wide 'Orders' export parsing -> order-confirmation evidence.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Evidence-only input to Verification Engine's corroboration signal, never a direct fact writer.

nodes.name: Dexie Persistence
nodes.responsibility: Implements every domain repository port over IndexedDB; the only code allowed to touch db.ts directly besides purge.ts.
sharedState: all 11 Dexie tables (see regressionGuards.test.ts's categorized allowlist)
nodes.criticalityRationale: The literal source-of-truth storage layer for the entire app (no backend exists) — a defect here is total or partial data loss/corruption, the maximum blast radius in this codebase.

ciRegressionGuards: the live schema's table list matches the reviewed, categorized allowlist exactly
only repository adapters and purge.ts's disclosed exception may touch db.ts directly (dependency-cruiser)

nodes.name: SnapshotPriceRepository
nodes.responsibility: Reads the static price-snapshot.json/price-history.json; the only class allowed to read price data.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Read-only over a static, out-of-band-produced asset; worst case is stale/missing prices, never a ledger-correctness defect.

nodes.name: Diagnostics recorder
nodes.responsibility: Observe-only instrumentation (session/write/read/decision/rule/perf events); no-op unless Developer Mode is on.
sharedState: diagnosticEvents, diagnosticCases — structurally forbidden from ever calling a business write method (CI-guarded, zero-tolerance)
nodes.criticalityRationale: No-op by default, structurally forbidden from writing business data; a bug here can at most produce a wrong diagnostic observation, never a wrong business outcome.

ciRegressionGuards: src/infrastructure/diagnostics/*.ts never calls a business repository write method or imports a business writer's writing functions

nodes.name: ImportPage
nodes.responsibility: Two-phase Extract/Distribute import UI; the primary way facts enter the system in practice.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Highest-traffic write surface in the UI, and has known, disclosed policy drift (re-derives some Verification Engine signals instead of calling it — ARCHITECTURAL_DEBT.md 3.2), so a change to Verification Engine does not automatically propagate here.

nodes.name: Dashboard / PortfolioDetail / Portfolios / TickerDetail pages
nodes.responsibility: Read canonical hybrid holdings + analytics + reconciliation flags for display.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Read-only presentation of already-validated data; a rendering bug is visible and doesn't affect stored state.

nodes.name: TradesPage / SellAllocationForm
nodes.responsibility: Entity-CRUD and lot-picking UI reading Trade directly (documented exception to reading via canonicalHoldings).
nodes.criticalityRationale: Reads raw legacy rows by design for lot-picking; a display bug is visible, but this is the UI surface closest to the FROZEN dual-writer tables so treat changes here as touching legacy-projection's shared state.

nodes.name: Diagnostics Center pages
nodes.responsibility: Session Recorder / Writer & Reader Trace / Case Detection UI.
nodes.criticalityRationale: Read-only, developer-facing observability UI; never reads a business repository for a business decision (DIAGNOSTICS_CENTER_SPEC.md Part 5.4).
